justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified — analysis opens with 'fundamentally changes the relationship between these contexts from independent to coupled' and BR-3 enforces aggregate boundaries. (2) Data ownership questioned — 'Available Stock' marked as NOT YET DEFINED, with explicit discussion of who owns it and whether Sales reads from Warehouse or consumes events. (3) TOCTOU/simultaneous-order race condition identified — BR-5 directly addresses lost-update prevention with a concrete test (two orders for 5 when stock=5), AC-3 codifies it. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — 'Stock Reservation' explicitly marked NOT YET DEFINED in ubiquitous language, escalation discusses reservation model as a missing concept. (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural coupling pattern — multiple escalations cover sync vs async integration patterns, with concrete options and recommendations. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-2 defines the invariant that orders with insufficient stock must not proceed to fulfillment.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough cross-context coupling analysis — identifies the integration as a fundamental domain model change, not a simple feature addition
TOCTOU problem clearly articulated in BR-5 with a concrete testable assertion demonstrating the race condition
Stock Reservation explicitly called out as NOT YET DEFINED in ubiquitous language — exactly the right framing
Available Stock ownership question raised clearly — marked undefined with discussion of whether it differs from StockLevel due to reservations, safety stock, or in-transit quantities
Four well-structured escalations covering blocking semantics, indicator staleness, integration pattern, and partial fulfillment — all with concrete options and recommendations
Edge cases are strong — covers reservation lifecycle on cancellation, stale indicator UX, bulk order depletion, and stock adjustment reconciliation
Low confidence score (45) is well-calibrated given the number of undefined business policies

trials.justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified — Bird calls out 'two previously independent bounded contexts' and the need for an anti-corruption layer. (2) Data ownership question raised — BR-4 states Warehouse owns stock truth, and escalation #4 asks who owns the new Reservation concept. (3) TOCTOU/race condition identified — BR-3 explains two concurrent orders seeing StockLevel=1, AC-3 tests it, and edge cases detail serialization requirements. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — BR-3 distinguishes reservation from raw check, and 'Stock Reservation' is called out as not defined in either context today. (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural coupling — escalation #3 and #4 address integration pattern and context ownership (though escalations are typed as 'ambiguity' rather than explicitly naming MJ, the architectural questions are clearly surfaced for architectural decision). (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-2 and BR-3 define the new invariants governing cross-context behavior.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough TOCTOU analysis — not just mentioned but modeled as BR-3 with a testable assertion and a dedicated acceptance criterion (AC-3)
Data ownership cleanly addressed: Warehouse owns StockLevel (BR-4), and Reservation ownership is escalated as an open question with three options
Edge cases are strong — reservation TTL/expiry, product not in Warehouse, backorder scenario, and partial order ambiguity all identified
Confidence level appropriately low (45%) given the number of unresolved ambiguities, with clear separation of high/low confidence areas
Escalations don't explicitly name MJ as the target for architectural decisions — they're typed as 'ambiguity' rather than 'architecture', though the content is clearly architectural in nature

trials.justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified — output calls it 'cross-context dependency that fundamentally changes both contexts' autonomy' and BR-5 enforces bounded context separation. (2) Data ownership questioned — output identifies StockLevel as owned by Warehouse and asks whether Sales reads directly or consumes events, with three integration pattern options in escalations. (3) TOCTOU/race condition identified — AC-5 tests concurrent orders for last unit, edge case explicitly describes it, BR-4 explains why raw StockLevel checks fail under concurrency. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — 'Stock Reservation' is marked 'NOT YET DEFINED' in ubiquitous language, BR-4 requires accounting for reservations, and a dedicated escalation covers reservation lifecycle/TTL. (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural coupling — multiple escalations cover integration pattern, failure modes, and reservation design, all clearly architectural decisions. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-3 defines the new order-stock invariant, BR-4 defines the reservation-aware check invariant.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough cross-context coupling analysis with BR-5 explicitly codifying bounded context autonomy as an invariant
TOCTOU identified both as an edge case and as the motivation for BR-4's reservation requirement
Stock Reservation marked as 'NOT YET DEFINED' in ubiquitous language — correctly flags the gap without presupposing a solution
Four well-structured escalations covering block semantics, indicator latency, failure mode policy, and reservation lifecycle
Confidence level of 40 is well-calibrated given the number of unresolved ambiguities
Edge cases cover reservation release on cancellation, TTL expiry, network partitions, and new products with no stock record
